text: Risk management is an essential process for any organization. It begins with identifying potential risks that could harm a project or business. These might include financial issues, technology failures, or natural disasters. Once risks are identified, companies develop mitigation strategies to reduce their impact. For example, they might buy insurance or improve safety procedures. However, not all problems can be prevented, so it is also important to create contingency plans. These are backup actions that will be taken if a risk becomes a real problem. Finally, risk management requires continuous monitoring. Risks can change over time, so businesses must regularly review their plans and adjust them if necessary.
